summary: Reviewer R5 completed a read-only RAG/search quality review of the requested memory registry, completion docs, retrieval/eval source files, and RAG status/data artifacts. Verdict is REVISE_REQUIRED because the governing completion conditions are directionally correct, but verification/status documentation and artifacts still allow local text-hash scaffolding to appear as healthy hybrid RAG and do not yet require generated answer-quality/memory-on-off lift evidence in the status layer.
decisions: Completion conditions require honest retrieval mode, real semantic dense retrieval, BM25/sparse+dense hybrid evidence, RRF/reranking contribution metrics, answer-quality evaluation, and memory-on/off lift.

Current implementation uses BM25/sparse, dense-vector/text-hash or dense-lexical fallback, RRF, and rerank reasons, but the dense provider is local_text_hashing_v1 with semantic_embedding_provider=false.
Current rag_eval_status is healthy with hybrid_ratio=1 and average_memory_lift=85.938, but caveats state it is deterministic canary only, not full Ragas/LLM-judge answer-quality evaluation.
Docs need to reconcile scaffold-health versus completion-health so text-hash hybrid cannot satisfy final RAG completion.
next_steps: Revise docs/VERIFICATION.md and completion docs to define scaffold healthy vs completion healthy for RAG proof/eval.
Require semantic_embedding_provider=true or a documented local multilingual embedding model before hybrid_contextual_v2 can count for completion.
Add required artifact fields for dense top-K, sparse/BM25, RRF, reranker/provenance/lifecycle contribution metrics, latency/candidate budgets, and generated answer-quality memory-on/off evaluation.
search_queries: rag retrieval hybrid semantic embedding provider answer quality memory lift
